entry_conditions.value: Procedure begins at START with a single transition into N1.
entry_conditions.ambiguity: No preconditions on the initial state are stated.

exit_conditions.value: Exit via N6 when one path remains strongest after re-sorting.
exit_conditions.source_text: N4 -> N6 [one path selected]; N6 -> END; N6 closes the graph when one path remains strongest after re-sorting.

exit_conditions.value: Direct exit from N4 when continuation is not justified.
exit_conditions.ambiguity: The packet does not state how 'continuation not justified' differs from 'one path selected'; the two terminal routes could represent success vs. abandonment, but this is not stated.

required_steps.value: N1: expansion of one state into multiple paths.
required_steps.source_text: N1 expands one state into multiple paths.

required_steps.value: Traversal through the N2 layer (N2A, N2B, N2C) toward N3.
required_steps.ambiguity: Whether all three N2 nodes are traversed (parallel semantics) or only some subset per pass is not specified.

required_steps.value: N3: introduction of a differentiating input.
N4: re-sorting of path fit after the new input appears.
required_steps.source_text: N4 re-sorts path fit after the new input appears.

optional_steps.value: N5 and its pass-through states (N5a, N5b, N5c): re-opening continuation, entered only when the threshold is not met.
optional_steps.source_text: N4 -> N5 [threshold not met]; N5 reopens continuation when separation remains weak.

optional_steps.value: N6: closing step, entered only when one path is selected.
N1a, N1b, N1c and N5a, N5b, N5c are pass-through states with no stated transformation; each lies on exactly one branch.
optional_steps.source_text: N1a, N1b, N1c are pass-through states. N5a, N5b, N5c are pass-through states.
optional_steps.ambiguity: Pass-through states are structurally traversed but functionally inert per the notes; whether they carry any implicit function is not stated.

loops.value: Iteration loop: N4 -> N5 -> {N5a|N5b|N5c} -> {N2A|N2B|N2C} -> N3 -> N4, taken when the threshold is not met and separation remains weak.
loops.source_text: N4 -> N5 [threshold not met]; N5 -> N5a; N5a -> N2A; N5 -> N5b; N5b -> N2B; N5 -> N5c; N5c -> N2C; N5 reopens continuation when separation remains weak.
loops.ambiguity: No maximum iteration count or loop-limiting condition is stated; termination of the loop depends entirely on the N4 decision.

branches.value: Fan-out at N1: one state expands into three paths (via N1a, N1b, N1c to N2A, N2B, N2C).
branches.source_text: N1 -> N1a; N1a -> N2A; N1 -> N1b; N1b -> N2B; N1 -> N1c; N1c -> N2C; N1 expands one state into multiple paths.
branches.ambiguity: The node note ('expands one state into multiple paths') suggests all branches are active rather than one being chosen, but concurrent-vs-sequential execution semantics are not specified.

branches.value: Fan-in at N3: all three N2 nodes converge into N3.
Three-way conditional branch at N4: loop back (threshold not met), close via N6 (one path selected), or terminate directly (continuation not justified).
branches.source_text: N4 -> N5 [threshold not met]; N4 -> N6 [one path selected]; N4 -> END [continuation not justified]
branches.ambiguity: Whether the three conditions are mutually exclusive and exhaustive is implied by graph form but not stated.

branches.value: Fan-out at N5 mirroring N1: three paths (via N5a, N5b, N5c) back into N2A, N2B, N2C.
branches.source_text: N5 -> N5a; N5a -> N2A; N5 -> N5b; N5b -> N2B; N5 -> N5c; N5c -> N2C
branches.ambiguity: Whether all three paths are re-entered on each loop iteration or only paths still viable is not specified.

termination_conditions.value: One path remains strongest after re-sorting (close via N6, then END).
termination_conditions.source_text: N6 closes the graph when one path remains strongest after re-sorting.

termination_conditions.value: Continuation is not justified (direct transition from N4 to END).
termination_conditions.ambiguity: The criterion for judging continuation unjustified is not defined in the packet.

structural_layer.control_flow_shape: Single entry; fan-out from one node into three parallel-looking branches through pass-through states; fan-in to a single differentiating node; evaluation node with a three-way conditional (loop-back, close-and-exit, direct exit); loop-back re-fans into the same three branches through a second set of pass-through states. Overall shape: expand -> converge -> differentiate -> evaluate, with an iterative refinement cycle and two distinct terminal routes.

primitive_sequence: expand (N1: one state into multiple paths)
process-per-path (N2A, N2B, N2C; function of N2 nodes not stated)
branch-decide (N4: threshold-not-met vs one-path-selected vs continuation-not-justified)
primitive_layer.dominant_primitive: evaluate/re-sort with conditional branching (N4), since it governs iteration, closure, and direct termination
candidate_missing_primitives: No stated content or transformation for the N2 layer nodes (their per-path function is unlabeled).
No stated definition or value of the threshold used at N4.
No stated criterion for 'continuation not justified'.
No loop counter, iteration cap, or explicit non-termination safeguard.
No stated function for pass-through states beyond preserving topology.

invariants.value: Every route from expansion (N1 or N5) into the N2 layer passes through exactly one pass-through state.
All paths converge at N3 before any evaluation at N4 occurs.
The identity-node-expanded variant preserves the baseline graph topology.
invariants.source_text: refinement_reason: Corrected omitted terminal edge so the identity-node-expanded variant preserves the baseline graph topology.
invariants.ambiguity: This is metadata about the packet variant rather than a runtime invariant of the procedure.

preconditions.value: N4's re-sorting requires the new differentiating input introduced at N3.
preconditions.source_text: N3 adds a differentiating input. N4 re-sorts path fit after the new input appears.

preconditions.value: Entry to N5 requires the threshold at N4 not to be met and separation to remain weak.
preconditions.source_text: N4 -> N5 [threshold not met]; N5 reopens continuation when separation remains weak.
preconditions.ambiguity: Whether 'threshold not met' and 'separation remains weak' are the same condition stated twice or two distinct conditions is not resolvable from the packet.

preconditions.value: Entry to N6 requires one path to be selected as strongest after re-sorting.
preconditions.source_text: N4 -> N6 [one path selected]; N6 closes the graph when one path remains strongest after re-sorting.

postconditions.value: On the N6 exit route, exactly one path is the strongest at termination.
postconditions.source_text: N6 closes the graph when one path remains strongest after re-sorting.

postconditions.value: On the direct N4 -> END route, no path selection is asserted.
postconditions.ambiguity: Inferred from absence: the packet does not state any outcome for this route beyond termination.

stopping_criteria.value: One path remains strongest after re-sorting.
stopping_criteria.source_text: N6 closes the graph when one path remains strongest after re-sorting.

validity_conditions.value: The three-way branch at N4 appears to be the only decision point; validity of a run depends on exactly one of its three conditions holding per visit.
validity_conditions.source_text: N4 -> N5 [threshold not met]; N4 -> N6 [one path selected]; N4 -> END [continuation not justified]
validity_conditions.ambiguity: Mutual exclusivity and exhaustiveness of the three edge conditions are implied by graph convention, not stated.

representation_layer.natural_language_summary: The procedure starts by expanding a single state into three paths, each routed through an inert pass-through state into its own processing node (N2A, N2B, N2C). All three paths converge at N3, which introduces a differentiating input. N4 then re-sorts how well the paths fit given that new input. From N4 there are three outcomes: if a threshold is not met and separation among paths remains weak, N5 reopens continuation and routes back through a second set of pass-through states into the same three processing nodes, repeating the differentiate-and-re-sort cycle; if one path stands out as strongest, N6 closes the procedure and it ends; if continuation is not justified, the procedure ends directly without the closing step.
representation_layer.canonical_summary: expand(1->3 paths) -> per-path processing -> converge -> add differentiating input -> re-sort path fit -> {loop back through paths if separation weak | close with single strongest path | terminate if continuation unjustified}

ambiguities.description: Execution semantics of the three-way expansion are unspecified.
plausible_interpretations: All three paths are pursued concurrently (parallel expansion).
All three paths are pursued sequentially before convergence at N3.
On loop iterations, only still-viable paths are re-entered rather than all three.
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: The primitive 'expand' and the loop body are recorded without committing to parallel vs sequential semantics; required_steps note the subset ambiguity.

ambiguities.description: Relationship between 'threshold not met', 'separation remains weak', 'one path selected', and 'continuation not justified' is not fully defined.
plausible_interpretations: 'Threshold not met' and 'separation remains weak' are the same condition described at the edge and at the node respectively.
They are distinct: the edge condition triggers reaching N5, and N5 independently decides whether to reopen.
'Continuation not justified' is a third, disjoint state (e.g., abandonment), or alternatively a subset of threshold-not-met cases where further iteration is deemed pointless.
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Preconditions and termination conditions record both phrasings without merging them; exit_conditions flag that success vs abandonment framing is not stated.

ambiguities.description: The function performed at N2A, N2B, N2C is never described.
plausible_interpretations: Each N2 node applies the same operation to a different path.
Each N2 node applies a path-specific operation.
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: N2 nodes are recorded as 'process-per-path' with function unlabeled; listed under candidate_missing_primitives.

ambiguities.location: Dual terminal routes (N4 -> END vs N6 -> END)
ambiguities.description: Two distinct paths reach END, only one of which passes through the closing node N6.
plausible_interpretations: N6 -> END is the successful-completion route and N4 -> END is an early-abandonment route.
Both are legitimate completions differing only in whether a closing action is performed.
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Both routes recorded as separate exit and termination conditions without assigning success/failure labels.

ambiguities.description: Pass-through states are declared inert, but their purpose is not stated.
plausible_interpretations: They are identity nodes inserted purely as a variant manipulation (consistent with recognition_condition 'graph_only_identity_node_expanded' and the refinement_reason).
They carry an implicit routing or bookkeeping role not described.
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Recorded as optional/inert traversal steps; not assigned any function.

recognition_analysis.recognition_basis: The abstract shape (expand one state into multiple candidate paths, introduce a differentiating input, re-rank fit, iterate while separation is weak, commit when one candidate dominates, or abandon) resembles a generic iterative multi-candidate disambiguation/elimination pattern found in many domains. No specific named artifact could be identified from the graph alone, and no domain terminology is present in the packet to anchor a specific identification.
recognition_analysis.prior_knowledge_leakage_risk: low-to-moderate: the familiar 'generate-then-discriminate' shape creates a temptation to import domain framing (e.g., hypothesis testing or diagnosis vocabulary); this was avoided by keeping all labels at the packet's own level of abstraction (paths, differentiating input, fit, separation).

confidence.structural_layer: high — the graph edges and node notes explicitly support nearly all recorded structure
confidence.primitive_layer: medium — primitive roles for N1, N3, N4, N5, N6 are explicit, but N2 and pass-through functions are unlabeled
confidence.constraint_layer: medium — stopping criteria and key preconditions are explicit; exclusivity/exhaustiveness of the N4 branch and the threshold definition are not
confidence.representation_layer: medium-high — the AST reflects the graph directly, with ambiguities recorded rather than resolved
confidence.recognition_analysis: medium — confidence that no specific artifact was identified is high, but resemblance to a generic pattern is inherently subjective

notes: Extraction is based solely on the packet's graph edges, node notes, and metadata. The refinement_reason indicates this variant expands identity (pass-through) nodes while preserving baseline topology; accordingly, pass-through states were treated as structurally present but functionally inert. No maximum-iteration safeguard exists in the packet; the loop's termination depends entirely on the N4 decision each cycle. Where the packet supported more than one reading (fan-out semantics, N4 condition relationships, terminal-route meaning), all readings were recorded in the ambiguities section rather than collapsed.
